PARTIES WHO HAVE APPEARED IN EITHER ACTION ARE SUBJECT TO COURT'S JURISDICTION. SEE, E.G. HAMILTON V. ASBESTOS CORP. LTD (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 1127, 1147-48. Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Omar J. Krashna, Krashna Law Firm, 510-836-2999 for Plaintiff and moving parties' Cindy Yu and Toshiyuki Musha
Stanley C. Shen, Shen Law Firm; 415-661-6700 for Defendant Shawn Yu

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFs CINDY YU and TOSHIYUKI MUSHA's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint, Or In The Alternative, To Consolidate Matters is granted as follows: Motion to consolidate is GRANTED, and therefore motion for leave to file an amended complaint is moot. Parties who have appeared in either action are subject to Court's jurisdiction. See, e.g. Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp. LTD (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1147-48.
The prevailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Goulding; Not Reported
